Start integrating Clawdbot with Discord, Telegram and your favorite platforms today. ## OpenClaw Integration FAQs. Common questions about Clawdbot Discord, Telegram, and other platform integrations. OpenClaw currently supports Discord and Telegram integrations, with WhatsApp, Slack, and Signal coming soon. How do I connect OpenClaw to Discord? Create a Discord application, copy your bot token, add it to your OpenClaw configuration, and invite the bot to your server. Connect to Discord, Telegram, and other platforms from a single OpenClaw instance. Are OpenClaw integrations free to use? Discord, Telegram, and other platform integrations are completely free.

Step 1: Create a Discord Bot and Obtain Token. This is the foundation of the entire **OpenClaw Discord setup** process -- creating an Application and Bot on the Discord Developer Portal and obtaining the necessary authentication Token. ### 3.1 Create a Discord Application. ### 3.2 Create Bot and Obtain Token. **Important security reminder:** The Bot Token is equivalent to full access credentials for the Bot -- anyone who has it can control your Bot. Never expose the Token in public places (GitHub, Discord channels, screenshots). After obtaining the Discord Bot Token, the next step is to write it into OpenClaw's configuration so the Gateway can connect to Discord as your Bot.

# Connect OpenClaw to Slack, Telegram & More: Integration Guide. Your AI agents are more powerful when they are accessible from the tools you already use. This guide walks you through connecting OpenClaw agents to Telegram, Slack, Discord, and other channels — with exact commands, configuration steps, and troubleshooting tips. ## Why Connect Your Agent to Channels? An AI agent running in a terminal is useful, but an agent you can reach from your phone, your team's Slack workspace, or a Discord server is dramatically more valuable. Or having your content writer agent post drafts directly into a Slack channel where your team can review them in real time. Or running a community support agent in Discord that answers questions 24/7 without anyone on your team lifting a finger. Each channel has its own strengths, and you can enable as many as you need for a single agent. Telegram, Slack, and Discord require a one-time setup to connect your bot tokens.

title: 🦞 Discord - OpenClaw
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# Discord. OpenClaw connects to Discord as a bot over the official Discord gateway. DMs and guild channels are supported. Discord DMs default to pairing mode. Create a Discord application with a bot, add the bot to your server, and pair it with OpenClaw. Use a private server if you can; create one first (**Create My Own > For me and my friends**) if needed. - ### Create a Discord application and bot. In the Discord Developer Portal, click **New Application** and name it (for example "OpenClaw"). Open **Bot** in the sidebar and set the **Username** to your agent's name. If the bot will post in threads — including forum or media channel workflows that create or continue a thread — also enable **Send Messages in Threads**. If your host is blocked or rate-limited by Discord's startup application lookup, set the application/client ID from the Developer Portal so startup can skip that REST call: `channels.discord.applicationId` for the default account, or `channels.discord.accounts..applicationId` per bot.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw: Scheduled tasks
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

Cron is the Gateway's built-in scheduler. It persists jobs, wakes the agent at the right time, and can deliver output to a chat channel, a webhook, or nowhere. `openclaw cron create "2027-02-01T16:00:00Z" \ --name "Reminder" \ --session main \ --system-event "Reminder: check the cron docs draft" \ --wake now \ --delete-after-run`. `openclaw cron listopenclaw cron get <job-id>openclaw cron show <job-id>`. `openclaw cron runs --id <job-id>`. Cron task reconciliation is runtime-owned first, durable-history-backed second: an active cron task stays live while the cron runtime still tracks that job as running, even if an old child session row still exists. Once the runtime stops owning the job and a 5-minute grace window expires, maintenance checks persisted run logs and job state for the matching `cron:<jobId>:<startedAt>` run. To require both conditions, use croner's `+` day-of-week modifier (`0 9 15 * +1`), or schedule on one field and guard the other in your job's prompt or command. On `cron edit`, removes the per-job model override so the job follows normal cron model precedence (stored cron-session override, else agent/default model).

title: 🦞 How to Use Cron Jobs in OpenClaw to Automate Nightly ...
content:

# How to Use Cron Jobs in OpenClaw to Automate Nightly Tasks Without Hitting Rate Limits. Scheduling OpenClaw crons overnight preserves your rolling quota window for daytime use. Here's how to set up nightly automation with smart scheduling. If you run OpenClaw automations during working hours, you’ve probably run into this: requests start failing mid-workflow, dashboards fill with error logs, and workflows stall right when you need them most. The fix isn’t a bigger plan — it’s scheduling cron jobs to run overnight, when your rolling quota window is clear. That means there’s no midnight cutoff to wait for — your quota tracks the past N minutes or hours at any given moment. Shift your heavy workloads to 2–3 AM and those requests expire from the window before your team arrives. This guide walks through how to set up nightly cron jobs in OpenClaw, schedule them intelligently, and build in the error handling that overnight automation requires. ## How OpenClaw’s Rolling Quota Windows Work.

# OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide. Diagram of OpenClaw Cron scheduled task automation execution. What Is OpenClaw's Cron? If you're familiar with Linux's `crontab`, OpenClaw's Cron is conceptually similar -- it automatically executes tasks at specified times. But the key difference is: traditional crontab executes fixed Shell commands, while OpenClaw's Cron executes **natural language instructions**, with the AI agent autonomously deciding how to complete them. `"Every day at 9 AM, check server status and send a summary to Telegram"`. The latter doesn't require you to write scripts in advance -- the agent will decide which check commands to execute, how to organize the results, and how to format the report on its own. `openclaw cron add --schedule "0 9 * * *" --message "Check the server's CPU and memory usage. If a Cron task's execution time exceeds this value, the Gateway will forcibly terminate the connection, causing the task to be truncated -- the agent may have completed 80% of the work, but cannot return results due to the timeout.
